project.title: Differential MAPK Phosphorylation of Connexin43
project.projectDescription: MAPK family members are known to phosphorylate the c-terminal tail of connexin43 (cx43), however little is known about the preference of different MAPKs for specific cx43 phosphorylation sites. We utilized mass spectrometry to determine the cx43 sites phosphorylated in kinase assays by Erk, JNK, and p38.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: In vitro kinase assays were reduced with dithiothreitol, alkylated with iodoacetamide, and subjected to the sp3 protocol with on bead tryptic digestion. Peptides were eluted with HEPES, lyophilized, and labeled with TMTpro.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw mass spectral data files were processed with Proteome Discoverer version 2.5 (ThermoFisher Scientific) and searched against a custom database with the connexin43 c-terminal tail and the human SwissProt database using Mascot version 2.8 (Matrix Science)

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Department of Biological Engineering, Koch Institute for Integrative Cancer Research, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, MA, USA
labPIs.name: Forest Michael White

references.referenceLine: Latchford LP, Perez LS, Conage-Pough JE, Turk R, Cusimano MA, Vargas VI, Arora S, Shienvold SR, Kulp RR, Belverio HM, White FM, Thévenin AF. Differential substrate specificity of ERK, JNK, and p38 MAP kinases toward Connexin 43. J Biol Chem. 2025:108178

Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: The top 15 most intense precursor ions were selected and isolated with an isolation width of 0.4 m/z.
Comment[Separation]: Peptides were separated with a 140 min gradient with 70% acetonitrile in 0.2 M acetic acid.
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: MS/MS acquisition was performed at a resolution of 70,000.
